what_it_does: Defines the 'space' axis — a typed view over the strict 8px spacing scale (--s-* tokens in colors_and_type.css) — and registers it on window.CV_AXES. It enumerates 14 spacing steps (s0 through s24, 0px→96px), grouped into inner/between/layout, each pointing at its spacing token with an informational meta.px.
observations: IIFE guarded on window.CV_AXES; errors and returns if axis-core hasn't loaded.
Step ids skip numbers (s7 then s9, s9 then s12…) — the numeric suffix loosely tracks the px/4 ratio but isn't strictly sequential.
Values built by mapping: id, label ('16px' style), token (s-0…), zero:true when px===0, group by index (0-3 inner, 4-7 between, else layout), meta.px.
Header states meta.px is informational only — the token remains the source.
standouts: Explicit note that meta.px is informational only and the token is authoritative — a clear single-source stance.
The 8px-rhythm framing makes the spacing scale a generative axis like size/motion.
Three usage groups (inner / between siblings / layout) give consumers semantic buckets over a raw ramp.
conventions: Same CV_AXES.make/register pattern as the other axes.
Single-home discipline: values reference --s-* tokens; meta.px explicitly non-authoritative.
concerns: The px numbers are inlined alongside token names; meta.px is labelled informational, but if --s-* token values change these numbers drift silently — mitigated by the explicit 'token is the source' note but still a second copy of the value.
Step id numbering is irregular (s7→s9→s12…) which could confuse consumers expecting contiguous ids.
notes: The 'space' axis = the typed 8px spacing ramp (--s-* tokens, 0→96px) grouped inner/between/layout; meta.px is informational, the token is the source; default s4 (16px).
questions: Why are step ids non-contiguous (s7 then s9, s9 then s12)? Do the suffix numbers correspond to a multiplier the consumer relies on, or are they just labels?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header describes a typed view over the 8px --s-* scale with informational meta.px, and the code builds exactly that ramp.
apparent_themes: Axis as a typed/ordered view over existing spacing tokens.
Single source of truth (the --s-* tokens), meta.px explicitly secondary.
Semantic grouping of a raw ramp by usage.
intention_signals: To offer 'the spacing steps the system provides' as one queryable axis over the 8px token scale, so all gaps/padding/margins dial from one home rather than each consumer picking arbitrary spacing.
contribution: Contributes the 'space' dimension of the axis model — the typed, grouped 8px spacing ramp over --s-* tokens with informational px metadata.
summary_for_embedding: A JS IIFE registering the 'space' axis on window.CV_AXES as a typed view over the 8px --s-* spacing tokens (s0→s24, 0-96px) grouped inner/between/layout, with informational meta.px.
intention_for_embedding: To give the system's 8px spacing scale one home as a generative axis so gaps, padding, and margins all dial from the same token ramp.
